tldr: Swap hosted Algolia for self-hosted (or Meilisearch Cloud) Meilisearch. Index data and most settings map cleanly; ranking semantics, replicas, and analytics differ enough to require re-tuning and a relevance QA pass.
why_switch: OSS, self-hostable — eliminates per-search vendor pricing at scale
Predictable cost: server bill instead of records + operations metering
Strong typo-tolerance and instant search out of the box
Simpler REST API and smaller mental model than Algolia's settings surface
No high-water-mark commit tier — capacity is whatever you provision
Permissive license + control over data residency and infra
Lower lock-in — easy to migrate away again if needed
what_to_export: Index records (full document payloads per index)
Index settings: customRanking and ranking criteria
Stop words and dictionaries (compounds, plurals, segmentation)
API keys and ACL scopes (search-only, admin, secured keys)
Analytics events / click & conversion tracking config (Insights)
InstantSearch / front-end UI configuration and templates
export_method: Use the Algolia API or `algolia` CLI to dump each index: records via browse, plus settings, synonyms, and rules endpoints. Save as JSON per index. API keys cannot be exported as plaintext — re-issue equivalents on Meilisearch. Analytics history is not portable; archive reports separately. Verify record counts against the dashboard before cutover.
import_method: Run a Meilisearch instance (Docker or Meilisearch Cloud). Create one index per Algolia index, set `primaryKey`, then bulk-add documents via `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents`. Apply settings via the settings endpoint (searchableAttributes, filterableAttributes, sortableAttributes, rankingRules, synonyms, stopWords). Re-create API keys via the keys endpoint. A small adapter script is usually required; community tooling exists but verify currency. Dual-write during cutover and diff top queries before flipping traffic.

concept_mapping.target: rankingRules — different default order, fewer knobs
sortableAttributes + sort param at query time
No direct equivalent — partially covered by filters/boosting + app-side logic
API keys + tenant tokens for scoped access
No built-in analytics on OSS — bring your own (logs, ClickHouse, etc.); Cloud has limited analytics

effort: 1-3 weeks for a typical single-app migration; longer with heavy Query Rules, Personalization, or many replicas. Self-hosting adds ops work (sizing, backups, monitoring).
gotchas: Algolia is schema-less; Meilisearch infers types per field — mixed types in one attribute can cause filter/sort errors
Ranking formulas differ — relevance will not match 1:1; budget time for re-tuning rankingRules and weights
Replicas → sortableAttributes is conceptually similar but query shape changes (sort param vs separate index)
InstantSearch works via the instant-meilisearch adapter, but not every Algolia widget/feature is supported — test before swapping
Query Rules have no direct equivalent; merchandising/pinning logic likely needs application-layer code
No built-in click/conversion analytics on OSS — wire up your own pipeline if you relied on Insights
Self-hosting requires sizing RAM/disk for the dataset; Meilisearch keeps significant data in memory
Highlighting, pagination, and faceting response shapes differ — front-end response parsing will need updates

email.subject: Cancellation and data export request for our Algolia account
email.body:
Hi Algolia team,

We're migrating our search workload to self-hosted Meilisearch and want to wind down our Algolia account cleanly. Could you confirm the best path to export all of our indices — documents, settings, synonyms, stop words, and rules — and flag anything that needs support involvement versus what we can pull via the API ourselves?

We'd also like to schedule cancellation at the end of the current billing cycle once exports are verified. Please share the steps to disable auto-renewal, the deadline to confirm cancellation to avoid the next charge, and any data retention/deletion timelines after the account is closed. If there's anything to know about commit tiers or remaining credits before we cancel, please flag it.

Aiming to wrap within two weeks. Thanks for the service.

Best,
[Name]
